economic_notes: Comprehensive Settlement Agreement with Canada and Manitoba re flooding under negotiation. Water and sewer project completed at cost of $20,624,129. New school construction in progress at $10,569,317. 20-unit housing project in progress at $7,198,355. COVID-19 impacts noted.
notes: Audit is qualified due to absence of budget information in the financial statements (not a going concern issue). Consolidated entities include Health Authority and CMHC Housing Authority. ISC funding figure of $2,116,900 is from Note 13 (ISC revenue operating only); Health Canada ($1,626,369) and CMHC ($91,534) are separate. Total government transfers $3,834,803 are all federal (no provincial transfers in 2020). Large flooding-related accountable advance of $1,336,284 appears on both sides of the balance sheet (due from Province of Manitoba and due to ISC - Accountable Advance - Flood), indicating unresolved flood reoccupation project. Net debt of $(879,077) is negative meaning net debt position. Segmented expenses for economic development show a negative expense of $(21,700) due to recoveries. expenses_administration mapped to Band Government segment; expenses_infrastructure mapped to Public Works segment.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
